description_long: This is a tall, rectangular stone obelisk with a weathered, aged appearance, likely constructed from large stone blocks. The front face features a prominent relief carving of a stylized figure or symbol, adding a historical or cultural element to its design. The stone surface is rough and uneven, with visible chisel marks and discoloration from exposure to the elements. The top of the obelisk is flat and slightly beveled, with some dark staining and a hint of moss or lichen along the edges. The sides and back are plain, showing the block construction and natural wear, while the base is simple and unadorned, blending seamlessly with the shaft. This obelisk would typically be found in a public square, park, or near a historical site in an urban environment.
description: A tall, weathered stone obelisk with a carved relief on the front and a rough, blocky texture throughout. The top is flat and slightly beveled, while the sides and back are plain and aged.
description_view_0: Front view showing a prominent relief carving and the rough, aged stone surface.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying plain, blocky stonework with visible weathering and no ornamentation.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the rectangular profile and the rough, unadorned stone texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with similar plain stonework and visible block seams.
attribute_ornamentation: A relief carving is present on the front face, depicting a stylized figure or symbol in a somewhat eroded, classical style.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple extension of the shaft, rectangular and unadorned, with no visible steps or decorative features. It appears to be made from the same stone as the rest of the obelisk.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and slightly beveled, with some dark staining and minor weathering but no unique decorations or features.
